message:
chore(beads): close deep code audit bd-17z7 and add CrimsonCrane audit bead bd-2zuz

- bd-17z7: mark as closed (completed parent closeout — fixed
  hardware_planner capacity vs fallback semantics; rch check+clippy pass)
- bd-2zuz: add new closed bead for CrimsonCrane deep code audit lane
  covering replay, federation, claims, and remote modules (fixed
  REMOTECAP expiry boundary fail-closed semantics with regression test)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-175t","title":"[test infra] Unblock cx_first_api_gate on rch workers missing ast-grep","description":"Why: rch validation reproduces deterministic failure in tests/cx_first_api_gate.rs (6/6 failed) because workers do not have ast-grep binary in PATH; local env passes only when ast-grep is installed. This stalls reproducible gate validation across worker fleet.\n\nScope:\n- Make tools/lints/cx_first_policy.rs resilient when external ast-grep binary is unavailable on workers.\n- Preserve AST-level matching guarantees (no comment/string false positives).\n- Keep existing policy semantics (violations, exceptions, expiry handling, artifacts) unchanged.\n\nAcceptance Criteria:\n-  exits 0 on workers without ast-grep.\n- Existing cx-first policy unit tests continue to pass and still catch violations/expired exceptions.\n- Verification artifacts written under artifacts/section_10_15/<issue-id>/.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlueLantern","created_at":"2026-02-22T17:45:06.598917745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T17:55:56.449118781Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T17:55:56.449096089Z","close_reason":"Completed: syn fallback for missing ast-grep implemented + rch post-fix gate passes (7/7)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["infra","section-10-15","tests"]}

 {"id":"bd-17mb","title":"[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks).","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unsupported major versions and missing required features hard-fail activation; version comparisons are semantic, not lexical; negotiation decisions are logged.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/manifest_negotiation.md`, `tests/conformance/manifest_negotiation_fail_closed.rs`, `artifacts/10.13/manifest_negotiation_trace.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-17mb/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-17mb/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks).", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:52.450176581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:32:31.228575187Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:32:31.228546493Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17mb","depends_on_id":"bd-1nk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:12.801157459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2zo1","title":"[10.21] Integrate BPET with ATC for privacy-preserving federated temporal intelligence exchange.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Why This Exists:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Task Objective:
Integrate BPET with ATC for privacy-preserving federated temporal intelligence exchange.

Acceptance Criteria:
- BPET exports anonymized trajectory summaries and consumes federated temporal priors without raw longitudinal leakage; contracts are verifier-checkable and versioned.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/federation/bpet_atc_bridge.rs`, `tests/integration/bpet_atc_temporal_interop.rs`, `artifacts/10.21/bpet_atc_exchange_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-2zo1/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.

 {"id":"bd-2zz","title":"[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md

Spec, guard script, and verification artifacts created.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-1","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zz","depends_on_id":"bd-1j2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:10.617269548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3014","title":"[10.15] Integrate canonical remote named-computation registry (from `10.14`) for control-plane distributed actions.","description":"## Why This Exists
Hard Runtime Invariant #6 from Section 8.5 requires a remote effects contract: all distributed actions must go through a named-computation registry that rejects unknown computation identifiers fail-closed. Section 10.14 (bd-ac83) built the canonical remote named-computation registry as a core asupersync primitive. This bead integrates that canonical registry into franken_node's control-plane layer so that distributed actions (remote health probes, cross-node rollout coordination, distributed fencing token operations, remote migration steps) use the same registry semantics. The key constraint is "no divergent registry behavior" — the product layer must not create a parallel registry or bypass the canonical one. Unknown computation names must fail closed with a stable error class, not silently fall through to a default handler.

## What This Must Do
1. Author `docs/integration/control_remote_registry_adoption.md` defining:
   - Which control-plane distributed actions exist (from bd-2177 workflow inventory).
   - How each maps to a named computation in the canonical registry (computation name, input schema, output schema, timeout, retry policy).
   - The fail-closed contract: any computation name not registered returns `RemoteComputationUnknown` error (stable error class, not a generic error).
   - The prohibition on divergent registries: no module may maintain a parallel name->handler map.
2. Integrate the canonical registry into control-plane modules:
   - `crates/franken-node/src/connector/` modules that perform remote operations must look up computation handlers through the canonical registry.
   - Add registration of control-plane computations at startup (lifecycle.rs init phase).
   - Wire the registry's fail-closed rejection into error handling paths.
3. Implement `tests/conformance/named_remote_computations.rs` that:
   - Asserts all control-plane remote operations use the canonical registry.
   - Attempts to invoke an unregistered computation name and asserts `RemoteComputationUnknown` error.
   - Asserts no divergent registry exists in the crate (module scan).
4. Generate `artifacts/10.15/remote_registry_adoption_report.json` with: registered computation names, usage sites, unregistered-name rejection test results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Control-plane paths use the same canonical registry semantics as 10.14; unknown names fail closed with stable error class; no divergent registry behavior is introduced.
- Every remote operation in the control-plane is registered by name in the canonical registry.
- The `RemoteComputationUnknown` error class has a stable error code that does not change across versions.
- No module under `crates/franken-node/src/connector/` maintains its own computation name->handler mapping.
- The adoption report is consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate computation registration, lookup, and fail-closed rejection with mock registry.
- **Integration tests**: Start the connector, register computations, invoke each registered computation, and assert success. Invoke an unregistered name and assert fail-closed.
- **Conformance tests**: Scan all modules for any `HashMap<String, Handler>` or similar patterns that could be a parallel registry.
- **Adversarial tests**: Attempt to register a computation with a name collision; assert rejection. Attempt to bypass the registry by directly constructing a remote call; assert compile-time or runtime rejection.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `RMT-001` (computation registered), `RMT-002` (computation invoked), `RMT-003` (unknown computation rejected), `RMT-004` (divergent registry detected). Include computation_name, caller_module, and trace correlation ID.

## Expected Artifacts
- `docs/integration/control_remote_registry_adoption.md`
- `tests/conformance/named_remote_computations.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/remote_registry_adoption_report.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3014/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3014/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-ac83 (10.14 — canonical remote named-computation registry implementation)
- **Downstream**: bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Control-plane paths use the same canonical registry semantics as `10.14`; unknown names fail closed with stable error class; no divergent registry behavior is introduced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:00.138232005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:01:43.741885689Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:01:43.741856054Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3014","depends_on_id":"bd-ac83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T14:59:47.584707824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
